parameters.description: An internal parameter to provide information about the starting location of the simulation. The specified text will be visible in the response of the status endpoint.
parameters.name: startedFrom

parameters.name: timeout

requestBody.description:
Specify a set of simulation options with values that will override the initial simulation options. </br>
Specify a set of input parameters with values to be provided for the simulation. </br>
Specify a set of output parameters to be obtained after the simulation. If no output parameters are specified, all initialized values are returned.

get.summary: Get the list of all currently running simulations including queued simulations that are placed in a waiting line.

parameters.description: Specify the ID of a simulation.
parameters.name: simulationId

requestBody.description:
Specify the name-value map of simulation variables to set for the current simulation. </br>
variables (mandatory) - Specify the name-value map of simulation variables </br>
context (optional) - Specify the ID of a target context. </br>
propertyPath (optional) - Specify the property path of a target.

post.summary: Set the value(s) for one or more simulation variables.

parameters.description: Specify the ID of a simulation.
parameters.name: simulationId

requestBody.description:
Specify a signal name or ID, a target name, a context ID, and a set of signal parameters with values. </br>
signalName (mandatory) - Specify the name of a signal. </br>
signalId (optional) - Specify element ID of the signal. Locating signal by ID is faster than by name. </br>
target (optional) - Specify the name of a target. </br>
context (optional) - Specify the ID of a target context. </br>
parameters (optional) - Specify a set of signal parameters with values.

post.summary: Send a signal to the specified simulation.
